Now restart the daemon so it reconnects with the correct port:

```
tenex-edge __daemon
```

Or if it's already running in the background, kill it first — the new daemon will pick up the corrected config and connect to `59886`.
Fixed. Now restart the daemon so it picks up the correct relay URL.
how can I run with a different tenex-edge config? some env variable to point to a different tenex-edge config that the one in ~/.tenex-edge?
Already there: `TENEX_EDGE_HOME=/path/to/your/dir tenex-edge <command>`. Everything — config, keys, state.db, logs — reads from that directory instead of `~/.tenex-edge`.
and something launched via `tenex-edge launch` will respect that env variable even when ran through the agent harness hooks?

Yes, it's explicitly passed through. `tmux/launch.rs:68` loops over `["TENEX_EDGE_HOME", "TENEX_CONFIG", "TENEX_EDGE_BIN"]` and forwards each one that's set in the parent environment into the new tmux session via `env` — so the spawned agent and all its harness hook calls will see the same `TENEX_EDGE_HOME` you launched with.
